quotes.text: Someone on my team once showed me a productivity framework — color-coded, laminated, the whole thing — and I looked at it and thought, this is a document made by someone who has never had an actual deadline. I use a legal pad. I have always used a legal pad.
quotes.speaker: Oprah Winfrey
quotes.context: On-stage conversation with Arianna Huffington at the Thrive Global Summit, Los Angeles, November 2023
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Oprah's slightly skeptical-of-over-engineered-systems side is well documented, and her legal-pad preference is something she's touched on publicly. The giveaway is the phrase 'this is a document made by someone who has never had an actual deadline,' which has a dry crispness that's a register sharper than her usual generous, expansive tone.

quotes.text: There's a scene in the first act that I must have watched back — I don't know, fifteen, twenty times — not because anything was technically wrong with it, but because I kept feeling like the character knew too much too early, and I couldn't figure out whose fault that was. Mine, probably.
quotes.speaker: Christopher Nolan
quotes.context: Extended interview with film critic Mark Kermode for the BBC Radio 6 Music 'Kermode on Film' podcast, December 2023
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Nolan is famously obsessive about narrative information and the pacing of audience knowledge, and his self-critical mode in interviews is well established. The subtle tell is that he lands on 'mine, probably' — a rare moment of casual self-blame that's slightly softer than his characteristically deliberate, defended public persona.

quotes.text: We shipped a version of that feature internally for something like nine months before it touched a product. There were people who thought I was burying it. I wasn't — I just couldn't find the moment where it felt honest. When something feels like a trick, I'd rather wait.
quotes.speaker: Jony Ive
quotes.context: In conversation with journalist Steven Levy at the 2024 SXSW Design track keynote, Austin, March 2024
quotes.fake_note: I made this one up — Jony Ive is well known for extended internal incubation periods and his language around design 'honesty' is thoroughly documented. The tell is 'feels like a trick,' which is slightly more colloquial and blunt than his typically careful, almost liturgical vocabulary around authenticity.

quotes.text: I never lie, even to this day. Not even a little. Unless you count playing pranks on people, which I don't. That's comedy. Entertainment doesn't count. A joke is different from a lie, even if the difference is kind of subtle.
quotes.speaker: Steve Wozniak
quotes.context: From his memoir iWoz (2006), the Apple co-founder on his lifelong commitment to honesty and his exception for pranks

source.title: Steve Wozniak (10+ Sourced Quotes) - Lib Quotes

quotes.text: I know acting is a childlike endeavour but it's no license to be childish.
quotes.speaker: Jeff Goldblum
quotes.context: The actor reflecting on his craft in a Time Out interview

source.title: Jeff Goldblum: 'What do people quote at me? That is one big pile of shit' - Time Out

quotes.text: The speed of light is never going to go out of style. Gravity, lint, signs, time itself, is not going to go out of style.
quotes.speaker: Steven Wright
quotes.context: In a Salon Talks interview, explaining why he avoids topical jokes so his material stays timeless

source.title: "Gravity is never going to go out of style": Steven Wright on the secret to comedy longevity - Salon.com
